text: Long BTC this week. Spot ETF inflows are back and funding is still flat, so I like the upside into Friday.

expected.thesis: ETF inflows and neutral funding suggest room for upside into the end of the week. The trade is a short term momentum play.

text: Short SOL today after the bridge exploit. Liquidity is thin and I expect forced selling into the close.

expected.thesis: An exploit often triggers forced selling and risk aversion, which can pressure price on the day. This is a short term downside play.

text: Buying AAPL for the next month. The new buyback authorization should support the stock and earnings guidance looks conservative.

expected.thesis: A larger buyback and conservative guidance can create a tailwind for the stock over the next few weeks. This is a near term support trade.

text: Short TSLA over the next 3 months. Price cuts are pressuring margins and demand looks soft into Q4.

expected.thesis: Ongoing price cuts and soft demand can compress margins, which typically weighs on valuation. This is a medium term downside view.

text: Long GC (gold) into the next quarter. Real yields are rolling over and I expect more rate cut talk.

expected.thesis: Falling real yields and growing rate cut expectations can support gold over the next quarter. This is a medium term macro trade.

text: Short CL for the week. OPEC signaled higher output and the market is still long.

expected.thesis: Higher output guidance can pressure crude, especially with crowded positioning. This is a short term downside setup.

text: I am buying YES on PRES24. Polling momentum has flipped and I think the incumbent wins.

expected.thesis: Polling momentum suggests a higher probability of the incumbent winning, so YES shares are attractive. This is a conviction trade.

text: Taking NO on RATE25. I do not think the Fed cuts by July given sticky services inflation.

expected.thesis: Sticky inflation reduces the chance of a near term cut, so NO shares should be undervalued. This is a macro probability trade.

text: Breaking: MRNA gets FDA approval for its RSV vaccine. This should be bullish for MRNA over the next month.

expected.thesis: Regulatory approval expands revenue potential and often drives near term re-rating. The news implies a long trade.

text: SEC announces a new probe into COIN trading practices. I expect downside as legal risk rises.

expected.thesis: A fresh regulatory probe increases legal risk and can pressure valuation. The news implies a short trade.

text: ETH looks interesting here, but I am just watching for now.
Watching MSFT and GOOG, but the only actionable idea is long MSFT into earnings next week.

expected.thesis: MSFT is the only actionable idea and the trade is into next week earnings. This is a short term event trade.

text: NVDA vs AMD: I prefer NVDA. Long NVDA for the next month as data center demand stays hot.

expected.thesis: Preference is clearly stated for NVDA and the trade is long into the next month. Demand strength is the driver.

text: Pair trade idea: long NVDA and short AMD as the gap widens.
Anyone think oil is going higher this week? I am on the sidelines.
Long ETH for a month. Staking yields are rising and L2 activity is picking up.

expected.thesis: Improving staking yields and stronger L2 activity support a month long upside view. This is a medium term crypto trade.

text: Short DOGE. The meme cycle is fading and liquidity is drying up.

expected.thesis: Fading meme momentum and weaker liquidity often lead to downside. This is a short term bearish trade.

text: I think NFLX will go up after subscriber numbers. Long NFLX into the report tomorrow.

expected.thesis: The post expects a positive reaction to subscriber numbers and calls for a long into the report. This is an event driven trade.

text: Short META for the next month. Ad pricing looks weak and I expect a reset.

expected.thesis: Weak ad pricing can pressure revenue expectations, supporting a month long downside view. This is a fundamental short.

text: Long NG into winter. Storage is low and weather models are turning colder.

expected.thesis: Low storage and colder weather expectations can lift natural gas prices into winter. This is a seasonal trade.

text: Short HG for the week. China PMI missed and base metals are rolling over.

expected.thesis: A weak PMI print can pressure industrial metal demand, making a near term short attractive. This is a macro downside trade.

text: Bet YES on BTC100. I think BTC clears 100k by year end.

expected.thesis: The author expects BTC to reach 100k by year end, so YES shares are the trade. This is a long horizon probability bet.

text: Breaking: JPM beats stress test with a huge buffer. Banks should rally, long JPM for the week.

expected.thesis: A strong stress test result can lift sentiment for the bank, and the text calls for a long into the week. This is a short term trade.

text: UAL cuts guidance due to fuel costs. Feels like a short setup.

expected.thesis: A guidance cut tied to rising costs often pressures airline stocks, implying a bearish trade. This is a near term downside view.

text: TSLA is a fascinating company, but I have no position and no trade here.
Hearing rumors about AMZN, could be big if true. Not sure yet.
BTC and ETH are both strong, but only trade is short AVAX after the unlock.

expected.thesis: The unlock is expected to add supply pressure, and the text calls for a short. This is a short term bearish trade.

text: Long SOL for the next quarter. Ecosystem activity is rising and dev grants are accelerating.

expected.thesis: Rising ecosystem activity and developer incentives point to sustained demand, supporting a next quarter long. This is a medium term growth trade.

text: Short SNAP into next week. Ad checks are weak and comps are tough.

expected.thesis: Weak ad checks and tough comps point to downside into next week, making this a short term short. This is a near term trade.

text: Long SI for a month. Gold strength usually pulls silver with it.

expected.thesis: Gold strength often supports silver, making a month long trade attractive. This is a relative value play.

text: Exchange announces it will list OP tomorrow. That usually pops the token, so long OP for the day.

expected.thesis: Major exchange listings often drive short term demand, and the text calls for a day trade. This is a catalyst driven long.

text: My strategy is to wait for a pullback in NVDA. No trade until then.
Scalp short BABA today if it fails 78. Tight stop above 79.

expected.thesis: A defined intraday setup calls for a short if resistance fails, making this a day trade. The thesis is tactical.

text: Maybe long ROKU if the market turns risk on, but I am not confident.

expected.thesis: The post still expresses a directional view to go long, albeit with low confidence. It is a conditional trade idea.

text: Long XOM. Energy is under owned and buybacks keep growing.

expected.thesis: Under ownership and buybacks support a bullish view on XOM, but no explicit timeframe is given. This is a directional long.
